mnemonics.title: Forest Definition
mnemonics.content: FOREST = FOrests REmain Separate Trees - emphasizes that trees in a forest are disjoint and separate from each other.

mnemonics.title: Edges in Forest
mnemonics.content: E = N - C (Edges equals Number of vertices minus Number of Components). For one tree (C=1), E=N-1.

mnemonics.title: Root Finding
mnemonics.content: In parent array, ROOTS have PARENT = -1 (or NULL). Remember: Roots have NO PARENT (-1).

acronyms.usage: Representation method to convert general trees/forests to binary trees
Forest that connects all vertices with minimum total weight in a weighted graph

visualTips.visualization: Draw multiple separate trees side by side with no connecting lines between them. A single tree looks like one inverted tree shape; a forest looks like several inverted tree shapes disconnected from each other.
Imagine an array where each index represents a node and the value at that index points to its parent. Roots point to -1 (nothing).
Take a tree with multiple children. The leftmost child becomes the left child in binary form. Siblings become right children in a chain.

analogies.analogy: Think of a forest as a grove of mango trees in an orchard - each tree stands alone but they are all called a forest (collection).
Like separate families - each family tree (genealogy) is independent with no connections between different families.
Like finding a path through all rooms in a building where some rooms are disconnected from others - you need separate paths for each group.
